title: hypericum tetrapterum var. neapolitanum
intro: Hypericum tetrapterum var. neapolitanum, also known as St. John's Spurge, is a perennial herb belonging to the St. John's Wort family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant has been linked to several health benefits, including reducing anxiety and depression, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Hypericum tetrapterum var. neapolitanum is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
